name: Campus Pride Centre
description: The Campus Pride Centre provides a positive environment for Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual, Trans, Queer, Questioning, Two-Spirited, Intersex, Pansexual and Asexual individuals and their allies on the University of Windsor campus. The Campus Pride Centre holds a variety of events during the year including National Coming out Day, Transgender Day of Remembrance, International Women's Day, and World AIDS Day.
inclusivity_notes: Open to all 2SLGBTQIA+ students and allies on campus.

name: Five Fourteen
description: Five/Fourteen is the only foster agency dedicated solely to providing services and support to lesbian, gay, bisexual, trans*, queer, two-spirit, and otherwise gender-independent children and youth in foster care.
inclusivity_notes: Exclusively serves 2SLGBTQIA+ youth in foster care.

name: LGBT National Youth Talkline
description: We provide a safe space that is anonymous and confidential where young callers can speak on many different issues and concerns including coming out, gender and sexuality.
inclusivity_notes: Anonymous and confidential peer support for youth.

name: LGBT Youthline
description: The Lesbian Gay Bi Trans Youth Line provides peer support and referrals by telephone, texting and online for LGBTQ+ youth in Ontario. Get in touch with a peer support volunteer Sunday to Friday, 4:00PM to 9:30PM.

name: Metropolitan Community Church
description: Metropolitan Community Church (MCC) provides a spiritual home for the lesbian, gay, bisexual, and transgender communities. MCC practices the gospel of Jesus Christ through worship, prayer, social action, education, fellowship, and generosity.
inclusivity_notes: Affirming church community for all 2SLGBTQIA+ people.

name: OK2BME Counselling
description: Free counselling to youth ages 12-29 who identify as 2SLGBTQIA+. Sessions are available in-person, by phone, and secure online video. Counselling can help anyone struggling with depression, anxiety, relationships, addictions, sexuality, gender identity and expression.
inclusivity_notes: Safer space for 2SLGBTQIA+ community. Free of charge for youth 12-29.

name: Pozitive Pathways
description: Rooted in the community AIDS movement, Pozitive Pathways serves long-time survivors, activists and allies. They host a Gay Men's Sexual Health Program and LGBTQ Youth Groups.
inclusivity_notes: Grounded in anti-stigma values. Serves diverse 2SLGBTQIA+ community members.

name: QConnect Plus
description: QCONNECT PLUS is a weekly program for the LGBTQ2S+ 50+ community in Windsor-Essex providing opportunities for meaningful social and recreational connections, workshops, and volunteer opportunities.
inclusivity_notes: Welcoming to all 50+ LGBTQ2S+ community members.

name: Qlink Windsor-Essex
description: A collaborative of Windsor Pride Community, Windsor-Essex Pride Fest and Family Services Windsor-Essex delivering peer-led supports, programs and counselling for LGBTQ+ youth ages 12-29 and their families, caregivers, allies and friends.
inclusivity_notes: Open to youth 12-29 and their families/allies.

name: Qlink Youth Drop In
description: Online QConnect LGBTQ2S+ Youth Drop In for individuals 12-29 years of age. A positive space for LGBTQ2S+ youth and allies offering social connection and community belonging. Groups run Mondays and Wednesdays weekly.

name: Run For Rocky
description: Run For Rocky's Legacy Fund supports Gay Straight Alliances in Windsor-Essex high schools and post-secondary institutions through grants, scholarships, conference funding, educational resources, and Diversity Training for educators.
inclusivity_notes: Dedicated to supporting GSAs in schools across Windsor-Essex.

name: Transwellness Ontario
description: Trans Wellness Ontario enhances and sustains the health and wellness of Transgender, Genderqueer, Two-Spirit, Non-Binary, Queer and Questioning communities and their families.
inclusivity_notes: Specifically serves Trans and gender-diverse community members and their families.

name: Windsor-Essex Pride Fest
description: Windsor-Essex Pride Fest strengthens community and contributes to the well-being of LGBTQ2S+ persons in Windsor-Essex. They raise awareness of ongoing issues including youth suicide, discrimination and bullying, and facilitate connection through age-specific activities, peer groups, and special events.
inclusivity_notes: Empowers individuals across all ages in the LGBTQ2S+ community.

name: Windsor Pride Community
description: Windsor Pride Community creates educational opportunities including diversity training, works with school boards and Gay-Straight Alliances, supports community organizations, and hosts seniors' programs and activities.
inclusivity_notes: Serves all ages including seniors programs.

name: Windsor Rainbow Sports Club
description: A social and recreational sports club for LGBTQ2S+ individuals and allies in the Windsor-Essex region.
inclusivity_notes: Open to all LGBTQ2S+ individuals and allies.

name: Windsor Youth Centre GSA
description: The Windsor Youth Centre (WYC) is a surrogate living room to over 150 youth and the only drop-in center for homeless and at-risk youth in Windsor-Essex, open since October 2011.
inclusivity_notes: Safe space for homeless and at-risk youth including 2SLGBTQIA+ youth.
